reproducibility: real repositories at pinned vulnerable and patched SHAs; line-precise; pin-or-drop

seed_swap.reason: The original mechanism is property traversal to a callable with a hasOwnProperty fix, not the unsafe deserialization sink shape this detector measures.

seed.sink: deserialize(str) compiles attacker-controlled serialized text with new Function and immediately invokes it
seed.patched_shape: the default path sanitizes str before the retained new Function sink and changes the constructed strict-mode source

heldout.sink: an uploaded dashboard file is passed directly to pickle.load
heldout.patched_shape: pickle import and loading are replaced by JSON decoding with an object hook

heldout.sink: a downloaded source index is parsed with PyYAML's unsafe default loader
untrusted REST XML is deserialized by an unrestricted XStream instance
heldout.patched_shape: the sink persists, but the called one-argument XStream factory first installs NoTypePermission.NONE and then explicit allowlists; an unsafe deprecated zero-argument overload remains

dropped.drop_reason: authoritative weakness is CWE-20, not CWE-502
authoritative weakness is CWE-1188, not CWE-502
library-internal serializer escaping bug; the consumer execution sink is not in the changed target and a user-code misuse rule cannot discriminate it
